title: parosela reclinata
intro:
Parosela reclinata, commonly known as the "Wild Strawberry Tree," is a plant species native to tropical regions of Africa. It is used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The therapeutic actions of Parosela reclinata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Note: The information available about Parosela reclinata is limited, and further research is needed to confirm its health benefits and bioactive constituents.
parts: Parosela reclinata has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and stems. The roots of Parosela reclinata contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. The leaves of this plant contain terpenoids, which have been reported to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities. Additionally, the stems of Parosela reclinata contain saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions and respiratory problems.
side_effects: Parosela reclinata can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while excessive skin exposure may cause redness, itching, and blistering. Overusing the plant can also disrupt liver function, leading to symptoms like jaundice, dark urine, and fatigue. Additionally, improper use can trigger seizures, tremors, and other neurological problems in rare cases.
